formula: If + Subject + Present Simple, Subject + will + Verb
formula_visual: If + [CONDITION, present tense], + [RESULT, will + base verb]
when_to_use: Use the First Conditional to talk about a real, likely possibility in the future and its probable result. The 'if' clause uses Present Simple (NOT 'will'), and the result clause uses 'will'.
when_to_use_ar: استخدم الشرط الأول للحديث عن احتمال حقيقي ومرجح في المستقبل ونتيجته المتوقعة. جملة if بتستخدم المضارع البسيط، وجملة النتيجة بتستخدم will.
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: The most common error is putting 'will' in BOTH halves of the sentence ('If it will rain, I will stay home'), because it feels logical to mark future time twice. English marks the future only ONCE, in the result clause — the 'if' clause stays in present tense even though it clearly refers to the future.

examples.en: If it rains, I will stay home.
examples.ar: لو نزلت شتا، هقعد في البيت

examples.en: If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
examples.ar: لو ذاكرت كويس، هتنجح في الامتحان
examples.structure: [If] + [you study hard] + [you will pass the exam]

examples.en: If she calls, I will tell her the news.
examples.structure: [If] + [she calls] + [I will tell her the news]

examples.en: We will miss the bus if we don't hurry.
examples.structure: [We will miss the bus] + [if] + [we don't hurry]

examples.en: If they don't finish on time, the boss will be angry.
examples.ar: لو ما خلصوش في الوقت، المدير هيتغاظ
examples.structure: [If] + [they don't finish on time] + [the boss will be angry]

common_errors.wrong: If it will rain, I will stay home.
common_errors.correct: If it rains, I will stay home.
common_errors.explanation: The 'if' clause uses Present Simple, never 'will' — the future is only marked once, in the result clause.

common_errors.wrong: If you will study hard, you will pass.
common_errors.correct: If you study hard, you will pass.
common_errors.explanation: Same rule — drop 'will' from the if-clause entirely.

common_errors.wrong: If it rains, I stay home.
common_errors.correct: If it rains, I will stay home.
common_errors.explanation: The RESULT clause needs 'will' to show a real future consequence — leaving it out turns this into a general habit statement instead (a different meaning).

practice_fill_blank.sentence: If she ___ (call) me, I ___ (tell) her the plan.
If it ___ (be) sunny tomorrow, we ___ (go) to the beach.

practice_fill_blank.hint: Result clause first this time — same rule applies regardless of order.
Negative if-clause still uses present simple, not 'will'.
Result clause: will. If-clause: present simple.

quick_rule: The word 'will' appears ONLY ONCE per sentence — always in the result clause, never in the 'if' clause. The 'if' clause always uses Present Simple, even though the whole sentence is about the future.
quick_rule_ar: كلمة will بتظهر مرة واحدة بس في الجملة — في جملة النتيجة دايمًا، مش في جملة if. جملة if بتستخدم المضارع البسيط دايمًا.
mnemonic: IF = 'Is it Future?' — yes, but only say WILL once, in the result. The condition itself just uses the plain present.
connect_to_speaking: Practice with your buddy: make 3 real predictions about this week using 'If... , I will...' — for example, weather plans, work deadlines, or weekend activities. Check each other for the double-will mistake.
